meta.notes: Fully rebalanced from consensus. All flows verified.

colSubs: ChatGPT Plus, Claude Pro, Gemini Advanced
Direct API calls to OpenAI, Anthropic, Google
M365 Copilot, SF Agentforce, NOW Assist, Canva AI

cells.ex: ChatGPT Plus ($20/mo, ~11M subs), Claude Pro, Gemini Advanced
cells.note: Largest revenue pool but discretionary. Heavy free-tier subsidy. High churn risk.

cells.ex: Hobbyist devs, indie hackers, students using API keys
cells.note: Small but pure. Every cent = tokens. ~95% frontier, ~5% open models.

cells.ex: Perplexity Pro, Canva Free/Pro, Monica AI, Wrtn free tier
cells.note: Real usage. AI native absorbs API cost.

cells.ex: Canva AI features, Grammarly, Notion AI, Adobe Firefly
cells.note: AI bundled free into consumer SaaS tiers. Retention play, not revenue driver. Adobe Firefly: growth flat at 10-11%.
Growing. Usage varies widely across seats.

cells.ex: Startups on APIs, dev agencies, Vercel AI SDK users
cells.note: Pure demand. ~65% frontier, ~35% open (AI natives buying Together/Fireworks inference). Fastest growing.

cells.ex: Cursor Pro/Biz, Replit Teams, Jasper Biz, Intercom Fin
cells.note: Strong PMF. SMEs adopt AI natives faster than SaaS AI.

cells.ex: HubSpot Breeze, Zoho Zia, Freshworks Freddy, Canva Teams AI
cells.note: Feature checkbox on renewal, low activation. HubSpot Breeze: no separate revenue disclosed = immaterial.
Standalone AI subs — higher activation, users chose to buy it.

cells.ex: Morgan Stanley, Klarna, Stripe, JPMorgan, Razorpay, Infosys
cells.note: LARGEST FUTURE TAM. Small today but 100% real. ~95% frontier, ~5% open. Goldman: ->$50-80B by 2028.
Real usage — AI natives solving specific enterprise workflows.

cells.ex: M365 Copilot (list $5.4B, net ~$2.5-3.5B after 40-60% discounting), GitHub Copilot ($1-2.3B — the one that works), SF Agentforce ($800M claimed, ~$250-350M real), NOW Assist ($600M, ~$400-500M real — cleanest reporter)
cells.note: COGS test: SF inference est. $6-12M on $800M "AI rev" = 98% margin = not real AI. NOW: 92-97%. MSFT only one with real margin impact (cloud margin -3pp). Vendors claim ~$52B total, real incremental is ~$9-13B across whole market (3.5-5% of enterprise SaaS). 0 of 10 vendors show AI-driven growth acceleration.

enterpriseReality.name: Salesforce
enterpriseReality.claimed: $800M Agentforce ARR (within $2.9B "AI+Data" that includes Informatica acquisition)
enterpriseReality.usage: ~50% of 29K deals are free/seeded. COGS test: inference cost est. $6-12M/yr on $800M = 98%+ "AI margin"
enterpriseReality.real: ~$250-350M real GenAI (rest is Data Cloud, Informatica, legacy Einstein)
enterpriseReality.flags: $2.9B bundles Informatica ($1.5B acq.) + Data 360 + Agentforce. Gross margin flat at 77% = minimal AI COGS. AWU metric criticized as vanity metric. <2% of $41.5B revenue.

enterpriseReality.name: ServiceNow
enterpriseReality.usage: Pro Plus premium est. 30-60% (UpperEdge). COGS test: inference cost est. $15-50M/yr = 92-97% "AI margin"
enterpriseReality.real: ~$400-500M (cleanest reporter — AI is a genuine premium SKU)
enterpriseReality.flags: Most honest of the group. But Pro Plus bundles non-AI features. Gross margin down 1.7pp (79.2->77.5%) — slight AI cost impact. Hidden consumption-based "assists" fees.

enterpriseReality.name: M365 Copilot
enterpriseReality.claimed: 15M seats x $30/mo = $5.4B list (verified, MSFT Q2 FY26 earnings)
enterpriseReality.usage: 35.8% active (Recon Analytics, 150K respondents). Only 5% of orgs past pilot (Gartner). 70% tried, only 8% kept choosing it.
enterpriseReality.real: ~$2.5-3.5B net (40-60% discounting per Citi/JPM). Cloud margin down 3pp (72->69%) — real AI infra cost.
enterpriseReality.flags: 3.3% of 450M installed base after 2 years. Market share FELL 18.8%->11.5% (Recon Analytics Jul->Jan). Auto-installing. Morgan Stanley: "wall of sorry."

enterpriseReality.name: GitHub Copilot
enterpriseReality.claimed: 4.7M paid subs (verified, MSFT earnings). GitHub total $2B ARR — Copilot est. $1-2.3B
enterpriseReality.usage: 42% market share of AI coding. 27-30% acceptance rate (not 55% — that was task speed). Lost $20/user/mo in 2023 (WSJ).
enterpriseReality.real: $1-2.3B — the ONE product where AI IS the product
enterpriseReality.flags: Genuinely works. But facing Cursor ($1B ARR), open-source alternatives. Microsoft added Anthropic models "at great expense to margins" (SemiAnalysis). Usage caps introduced mid-2025.

enterpriseReality.name: SAP
enterpriseReality.claimed: "2/3 of deals include AI" — Joule bundled free in RISE/GROW
enterpriseReality.usage: No separate metering. No AI revenue disclosed = immaterial.
enterpriseReality.real: ~$0-200M. Like saying "100% of iPhones include Siri"
enterpriseReality.flags: Cloud growth (23%) driven by S/4HANA forced migration (ECC end-of-life 2027), NOT AI. No analyst attributes growth to Joule.

enterpriseReality.name: Adobe
enterpriseReality.usage: 75% of Fortune 500 "adopted" but free tier inflates. Some cannibalizes stock photo revenue.
enterpriseReality.flags: Growth completely flat at 10-11% pre AND post AI. Firefly has moved the needle zero on top-line growth.

enterpriseReality.name: Workday
enterpriseReality.claimed: Not disclosed — "growing demand for AI SKUs"
enterpriseReality.usage: No AI metric = immaterial. Flex Credits model.
enterpriseReality.flags: Growth crashing: 21%->13%. AI has zero visible impact.

topConsumers.ev: Live dashboard: $1.3M/day spend, $475M/yr managed. 35%/wk growth. Gateway passthrough (not additive to provider totals).
4.7M paid subs, margin-negative (MSFT subsidises), $1-2.3B ARR. Revenue/price: 1.4-4.1T. Bottom-up: 471-800B. Midpoint ~1T.
$1B+ ARR, 40-60% COGS ratio. Revenue/price: 1-1.6T. Bottom-up: 400-800B. COGS: 680-980B. Midpoint ~800B.
Self-reported 8.4T/mo. Most reliable figure — transparent platform with public pricing. Gateway passthrough.
780M queries/mo, $200M ARR. Revenue/price: ~220B. Bottom-up: 91-130B (but multi-step pipelines inflate). COGS: 220-290B.
$240-265M rev but only ~60% AI-related. 50M registered != DAU (likely <2M). Revenue/price: 260-310B. COGS: ~192B. Bottom-up much lower.
$155M ARR combined. Agent sessions burn 50-200K tokens each. Revenue/price: 178-212B. COGS: ~148B.
47.7M DAU but most exercises pre-generated. $10-30M/yr AI spend (margin compression data). At $0.30-0.50/M: 110-183B.
$150M+ cloud spend, own models. Hard to decompose training vs inference. 212M cumulative (not DAU). Wide range.
800M AI uses/mo but most are tiny interactions (~1K tokens). "Trillion Token Club" = cumulative, not daily. COGS: 85-170B.
10M "AI users" but active AI DAU likely ~1M. $10/user/mo add-on. Revenue/price: 137-171B. COGS: ~106B.
6.5M MAU, free GPT-5. Revenue/price: 68-96B. Bottom-up: 9-32B. 200B/day would exceed entire $100M ARR.
$31.7M rev, 20M registered (not DAU). Revenue/price: 107-172B. Bottom-up: 10-23B. Midpoint of financial methods.
$190M ARR, 100K lawyers but DAU ~30-40K. Frontier-heavy = expensive per token. Revenue/price: 58-73B. COGS: ~50B.
10M registered (not DAU, likely 300-500K). $100M ARR (Manus). Revenue/price: 68-82B. Bottom-up: 8-15B.
1,300+ clients, $79.5M rev. Many conversations are rule-based, not LLM. Revenue/price: 131-175B. Bottom-up: 8-20B.
"700 agents replaced" = $21-28M equivalent, but AI spend much less. CS interactions are short (~2K tokens). Bottom-up: 0.7-30B.
42M+ MAU but most "AI" is traditional ML (matching, fraud), not LLM. LLM-specific: CS chatbot + internal tools.
OpenAI strategic MOU is forward-looking. Current LLM deployment: CS + product descriptions. $13B total rev.
53M MAU but AI feature adoption maybe 5-10%. Revenue/price: 27-68B. Bottom-up: 4.5-22B.
$800M claimed but bundled/relabeled. Realistic: 5-20K active agents x 100 interactions x 3K tokens. Inference maybe $20-50M/yr.

products.name: Microsoft 365 Copilot
products.source: Gartner: 35.8% active usage; only 3.3% of M365 base

products.name: Salesforce Agentforce
products.source: ~12% of customers have deals; community reports minimal usage

products.name: ServiceNow Now Assist
products.source: <20% of licensed Fulfillers actively use AI features

products.name: SAP Joule
products.source: "Ninefold growth" from undisclosed base. No separate AI SKU.

products.name: Workday Illuminate
products.source: Early stage. MIT: only 5% see real AI ROI.

products.name: ChatGPT Enterprise
products.source: Higher activation than bundled SaaS — users chose to buy it

products.name: ChatGPT Team
products.source: Team leads buy it intentionally. Moderate activation.

products.name: Claude for Enterprise
products.source: Enterprise API-heavy. Higher activation than average.

products.name: GitHub Copilot Business
products.source: 55% code acceptance rate. Devs actively use it.

products.name: Gemini for Google Workspace
products.source: Bundled into Workspace. Lower activation than standalone AI.

products.name: Cursor Business
products.source: Every keystroke = API call. Power users consume $5K/mo retail.

products.name: Harvey
products.source: Legal doc analysis = massive context windows. $190M ARR.

products.name: Replit Teams
products.source: Code generation is the product. Gross margin swings with LLM costs.

events.title: The GPT-4 Era
events.text: OpenAI dominates at $3.4B ARR. Anthropic still tiny ($200M). Open models (Llama 2) exist but lag far behind frontier quality. Self-hosted inference is niche.

events.title: GPT-4o + Claude 3.5 Sonnet + Llama 3
events.text: Three game-changers: GPT-4o halves prices, Claude 3.5 Sonnet disrupts coding, and Llama 3 closes the open-source quality gap. Open model tokens start growing fast.

events.title: The Mini Revolution
events.text: GPT-4o mini at $0.60/M output — 99.5% cheaper than GPT-4. But Llama 3.1 405B launches in July, closing the frontier quality gap. vLLM Docker pulls accelerate. Self-hosting becomes viable at enterprise scale.

events.title: Open Source Inflection
events.text: Llama 3.1, Qwen 2.5, DeepSeek V2.5 all strong. Self-hosted tokens now ~9% of global volume. Enterprises self-hosting for data sovereignty and cost. The "invisible token" economy emerges.

events.title: DeepSeek Shock
events.text: DeepSeek R1 matches GPT-4o at 1/10th the price — and it's open-weight. Triggers pricing pressure across all providers. Open model share jumps to ~20%. The question shifts: why pay for frontier?

events.title: The Enterprise AI Mirage
events.text: M365 Copilot: 15M seats, 35.8% usage. Agentforce, Now Assist: revenue booked ≠ used. Meanwhile open models quietly grow — banks, governments, sovereign clouds all deploying Llama/Qwen.

events.title: Anthropic Takes Enterprise, OSS Takes Volume
events.text: Anthropic captures 40% of enterprise LLM spend. But open models capture the volume story — 80T tokens/day with almost no model-layer revenue.

events.title: The AI Native Explosion
events.text: Cursor $1B ARR, Harvey $190M, Cognition $155M. Many AI natives use a mix of frontier AND open models, routing by cost/quality. litellm hits 97M downloads/month.

events.title: Today: Three Markets
events.text: Frontier APIs (OpenAI/Anthropic) win on quality. Open models (Llama/DeepSeek) win on cost and sovereignty. Traditional SaaS AI (Salesforce/Microsoft) wins on... marketing. The revenue gap between tokens served and tokens paid for keeps widening.
